title: Chapter Forty-Two: The Seventh Apostle's Eye
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

The Blood Swordmaster was fast.

Not fast enough.

Yeon-shin closed the distance in one breath, arriving at the man's back before the disguise had fully dropped. Even the man's own surprise could not match it -- the speed itself was different, refined through repeated insights, true energy efficiency honed to a razor's edge.

*However.*

The force wave emitted from his striking hand was not what he expected. The new dharma-imbued martial arts he had tried to unleash did not come. The old techniques were still crude, not yet meshed.

He struck with physical power instead. Even that alone was supernatural strength.

The blow landed on the back of the Blood Swordmaster's skull.

The man collapsed from the chin. His body twitched once, twice, and was still. Dead. A death throe convulsion and nothing more.

"With one move against such a--!" one of the Deep Martial Alliance captives blurted.

"Truly advanced martial arts!" Hyeon Yu-ryang exclaimed.

The words were meaningless. Yeon-shin's senses, reaching intuition, dissected the phenomenon in detail. He knew immediately. The dharma power techniques he had tried to weave together were still lacking the single bolt of inspiration that would fuse them into one.

*I'll achieve it soon.*

He did not get impatient. When the upper dantian communicated with heaven and earth, they said the realm of foresight sometimes came. He felt the new art was close. Perhaps even today.

He turned to Hyeon Yu-ryang. "The Blood Flame Sect has infiltrated the escort bureau. Can the Righteous Heaven Sword Family handle it?"

"Of course. Mount Pingding's matters are our matters. We can only thank Lightning Flash for finding this." He clasped his fists. A famous family's heir, trained in the proper handling of ugly affairs.

Then Hyeon Yu-ryang's gaze turned strange.

"Excuse me, but may I ask your age?"

"Sixteen."

"The right age. If you don't have a marriage arrangement, would it be alright to show you my younger sister? She's a very beautiful and kind child."

*Marriage.*

The thing furthest from him. A life of having children with someone and living together for a hundred years. Reachable only in dreams.

"I appreciate your words," Yeon-shin said, "but I lack time even to hold a sword. It would be hard to call me a good marriage prospect. Since my family met with annihilation."

Hyeon Yu-ryang's smile did not waver. Yeon-shin threw his gaze north.

It was time for the scattered Radiant Demon Wing to gather.

---

Hyeon Won-chang welcomed him among the clean pavilion buildings of the Righteous Heaven Sword Family with wounds still seeping blood through cotton bandages and a grin that split his face.

"I knew you'd be safe! You did really well, Sir Jeong!"

After a brief reunion, Yeon-shin asked the only question that mattered. "No internal injuries?"

"Fortunately not. Sir Jeong looks perfectly fine."

"Because I escaped to the outskirts and took a boat."

"Ah! Come to think of it, they said you caught those viciously evil Deep Martial Alliance youngsters. That too was really well done."

The scar on Ma Jin's chin rose slightly when Yeon-shin reached the seniors.

"You achieved merit even in that situation."

"How is the squad leader? The Seventh Apostle's neck...?"

"That demoness's body technique was a poor match for me," Ma Jin said. His eyes turned. "If Cheong Myeong had reached my level, he could have killed her."

"You call that an excuse?" Cheong Myeong grinned.

Baek Mi-ryeo grabbed Yeon-shin's shoulders and looked him over before nodding. "Healthy to an excessive degree. Your energy seems to be changing once again. Usually this is the age to build your vessel, but you keep advancing endlessly."

"Thank you, senior."

She had been acting like a sister for some time now. He turned slightly to remove her hands. Grateful, but still awkward.

"The Blood Flame Sect pursuit mission?" he asked Ma Jin. "The Seventh Apostle seems to have information about their headquarters."

"We wait. We plan to respond in agreement with the Righteous Heaven Sword Family. The Seventh Apostle's demeanour was strange -- they too seemed to have received extermination orders against us. From main fortress and headquarters perspective it's a local battle, but for us it's all-out war."

"Can we know the timing?"

"Their advantage is that we don't know that. We must counter-strike. We have a secret plan. The Righteous Heaven Sword Family's mechanism formation -- we won't be caught in a surprise attack."

*Mechanism formation.*

Strange techniques woven with the principles of great nature. Dwarf clan craftsmen from the main sect had come personally to build it.

The day darkened. Yeon-shin greeted Radiant Demon First Team, reunited with the Jeong family administrator and Little Divine Monk Gak Jeong, then entered his sleeping quarters.

He did not immediately fall asleep.

*Let's organize the oral chants.*

He sat cross-legged wearing moonlight as clothes. The meditation continued until daybreak. He did not move until the blanket of moonlight changed to sunlight.

---

After breakfast, Hyeon Yu-ryang's invitation arrived along with splendid silk clothes. The message was clear enough: his younger sister would be present.

Yeon-shin pinched the silk between thumb and index finger. Stared at it.

"Radiant Demon Wing!"

Ma Jin's voice, full of true energy. The door shook on its hinges. The atmosphere boiled.

Enemy raid.

He grabbed the Desolate Sword. The pitch-black scabbard shimmered in the morning light. He stepped out already fully prepared.

The seniors were in the courtyard. Hyeon Won-chang came running. Before Ma Jin could explain, a hidden weapon shot at Won-chang's head. He caught it without breaking stride.

"Interesting." Won-chang crushed the flying needle in one hand. "This time will be different." He glanced at Yeon-shin.

"Fire bombs exploded at the south gate," Ma Jin said, grinding the words. "They've destroyed the formation. They're completely mad."

"No regard for the imperial court or anything," Baek Mi-ryeo said.

"Did the Tang Clan really make these?" Cheong Myeong muttered.

They were watching the Seventh Apostle approach through the shattered back gate. The Blood Swordmasters and cultists spread behind her were tremendous in number. Two hundred, easily.

The Seventh Apostle walked with an elegant gait that commanded attention. She moved her lips slowly.

"No way. The Tang Clan is an orthodox sect only in name."

Her smile was seductive. Red.

Ma Jin stepped forward.

"You sow distrust. You, demoness. You don't need to try so hard. Desolate Sect watches all sects under heaven. It's always been that way."

"All the martial sects and noble clans under heaven. Desolate Sect. Hypocrisy that won't let go of its grip on the world."

"The evil sect's demoness has a long tongue."

"Our sect's blood flame will cleanse the world. See the blood world even in death."

*Is this how all those immersed in evil sects speak?*

The Seventh Apostle jerked her chin.

Simultaneously, the Blood Flame Sect's momentum surged like a military formation. The clash erupted -- steel on steel, energy waves clawing the earth. Ma Jin locked with the Seventh Apostle. Righteous Heaven Sword Family masters came running. Shaolin monks and Radiant Demon Wing mixed with Blood Flame Sect members, and the courtyard became a killing ground.

From the battle's opening, Yeon-shin captured the two supreme masters in his eyes. Sinister true energy stabbed at his skin. While Hyeon Won-chang blocked one Blood Swordmaster's blade, Baek Mi-ryeo cut past the man's throat.

Life-and-death battle. No room for face or righteousness in a group fight.

Yeon-shin cut off one Blood Flame Sect member's arm. Holding the Desolate Sword, he prowled through the melee like scattered leaves -- yet he did not take his gaze off Ma Jin and the Seventh Apostle.

Blood scent hung thick in the wind.

*There.*

Three Blood Swordmasters surrounded the Seventh Apostle and Ma Jin, their sword strikes only coming when blows clashed. They dispersed his attention, supported her flanks. Outstanding ones, even among sect swords.

"Perfect," Yeon-shin muttered.

He moved. Slowly. Twisting his body through the melee, his body technique approaching completion. Blood Flame Sect members' weapons brushed past him with a bewilderment they could not name. Martial prowess similar to blue-level was flourishing in him, step by step.

The closer he got, the more strongly the Seventh Apostle's energy stimulated his entire body.

*Was it dharma's voice?*

Intent newly guiding true energy arose. The ominous storm of her power was moving him to another realm.

In the gap where supreme masters' force waves swept the surroundings, Yeon-shin stepped in. The Blood Swordmasters had dissolved force waves while shielding the Seventh Apostle, and that created his opening.

Ma Jin's eyes widened. The Seventh Apostle's lips rose.

"Not knowing your place," she said.

Her left hand came -- casually, with tremendous density, spare energy thrown at a boy while she focused on Ma Jin. The three Blood Swordmasters directed their strikes only at Ma Jin.

To Yeon-shin, it was welcome. Her hand had already slowed enough.

The baihui acupoint at his crown went white-hot.

His head went numb.

Gak Jeong's fist and leg techniques, the Shaolin monks' dharma talks, and all the old techniques he possessed tangled and burst in his mind like lightning. The light net arising in his consciousness created new paths, and inspiration blazed through him to write five characters.

Demon Destroying Blue Steel Hand.

He created and extended the hand technique in the same breath. Divine energy gathered in the force wave -- a very faint blue light.

His palm struck the Seventh Apostle's casually extended hand and it bounced back. The dharma power dissolved her blood technique force wave with extreme compatibility. Wind gathered and distorted everywhere. His hand advanced straight through the wreckage of scattered energy.

At the end of the straight force wave containing pale blue light was her left eye.

He took it.

"Ahhh!"

The Seventh Apostle's scream tore through the battlefield. Blood seeped through gaps in her white hand as she clutched the ruined socket. Her slender body trembled.

"You -- youuu!"

She could not handle Ma Jin now. Tremendous body technique carried her back, creating distance in an instant. Ma Jin cut down the three Blood Swordmasters at once, but even he could not hide the shock on his face.

Everyone who began looking this way was the same.

Silence filling the hall amid the battlefield's clamor.

"You said blood world," Yeon-shin said calmly. "Now you'll only see half of it."

He shook his left hand once. The blood remaining on the back of his hand bothered him.

---
